rationale: Fingerprint `.split(STR).map(FN).map(FN)` — the canonical pattern is `t.split("\n").map(JSON.parse).map(n=>({name: n.<field>, ...}))` for JSON-lines output (docker/podman ps, volume, network, image, etc.). The bucket contains 63 variants that each read a DIFFERENT field (ID / Id / Name / Repository). A single `json_extract("ID")` pipeline only matches the ID variants (19/63) on real-world inputs. Workaround: feed a single JSON line that contains ALL plausible field names with the same value — `{ID:"x", Id:"x", Name:"x", Repository:"x", ...}` — so every JS variant extracts the same string regardless of which field it reads, and Rust's `json_extract("ID")` also extracts that string. Expected outlier: one docker generator uses a template-literal `\`${n.Name}=\`` which appends `=` and will `fail` — that's a legitimate shape-too-loose signal, not a fixture bug. Inputs deliberately avoid trailing newlines (JS `JSON.parse("")` throws because there is no try/catch in this shape).
